Submodules

news.admin module

Admin configuration and group permission setup.

news.admin.create_groups()[source]

news.api_views module

API views for the LNN News Application.

Provides REST API endpoints for retrieving approved articles based on user subscriptions

news.api_views.subscribed_articles_api(request, *args, **kwargs)[source]

Returns approved articles from subscribed publishers and journalists.

Parameters:

request (Request) – Incoming API request

Returns:

Serialized list of approved articles

Return type:

Response

news.apps module

class news.apps.NewsConfig(app_name, app_module)[source]

Bases: AppConfig

name = 'news'

news.forms module

Forms used in the LNN News Application.

class news.forms.ArticleForm(data=None, files=None, auto_id='id_%s', prefix=None, initial=None, error_class=<class 'django.forms.utils.ErrorList'>, label_suffix=None, empty_permitted=False, instance=None, use_required_attribute=None, renderer=None)[source]

Bases: ModelForm

Form used to create and update articles.

Return all media required to render the widgets on this form.

property media

Return all media required to render the widgets on this form.

news.models module

Database models for the LNN News Application.

This module contains: - Customer user model with role support - Publisher model - Article model - Newsletter model - Password reset token model

class news.models.Article(*args, **kwargs)[source]

Bases: Model

Represents a news aricle submitted by a journalist.

Articles must be approved by editors before being distributed to readers.

exception DoesNotExist

Bases: ObjectDoesNotExist

exception MultipleObjectsReturned

Bases: MultipleObjectsReturned

exception NotUpdated

Bases: ObjectNotUpdated, DatabaseError

author

Accessor to the related object on the forward side of a many-to-one or one-to-one (via ForwardOneToOneDescriptor subclass) relation.

In the example:

class Child(Model):
    parent = ForeignKey(Parent, related_name='children')

Child.parent is a ForwardManyToOneDescriptor instance.

author_id
body

A wrapper for a deferred-loading field. When the value is read from this object the first time, the query is executed.

created_at

A wrapper for a deferred-loading field. When the value is read from this object the first time, the query is executed.

get_previous_by_created_at(*, field=<django.db.models.fields.DateTimeField: created_at>, is_next=False, **kwargs)

class news.models.CustomUser(*args, **kwargs)[source]

Bases: AbstractUser

Custom user model with role-based access control.

Extends Django’s AbstractUser model by adding: - user roles - publisher subscriptions - journalist subscriptions

exception DoesNotExist

Bases: ObjectDoesNotExist

exception MultipleObjectsReturned

Bases: MultipleObjectsReturned

exception NotUpdated

Bases: ObjectNotUpdated, DatabaseError

ROLE_CHOICES = (('reader', 'Reader'), ('editor', 'Editor'), ('journalist', 'Journalist'))
articles

Accessor to the related objects manager on the reverse side of a many-to-one relation.

In the example:

class Child(Model):
    parent = ForeignKey(Parent, related_name='children')

Parent.children is a ReverseManyToOneDescriptor instance.

Most of the implementation is delegated to a dynamically defined manager class built by create_reverse_many_to_one_manager() defined below.

date_joined

A wrapper for a deferred-loading field. When the value is read from this object the first time, the query is executed.

editor_publishers

Accessor to the related objects manager on the forward and reverse sides of a many-to-many relation.

In the example:

class Pizza(Model):
    toppings = ManyToManyField(Topping, related_name='pizzas')

Pizza.toppings and Topping.pizzas are ManyToManyDescriptor instances.

Most of the implementation is delegated to a dynamically defined manager class built by create_forward_many_to_many_manager() defined below.

email

A wrapper for a deferred-loading field. When the value is read from this object the first time, the query is executed.

first_name

A wrapper for a deferred-loading field. When the value is read from this object the first time, the query is executed.

followers

Accessor to the related objects manager on the forward and reverse sides of a many-to-many relation.

In the example:

class Pizza(Model):
    toppings = ManyToManyField(Topping, related_name='pizzas')

Pizza.toppings and Topping.pizzas are ManyToManyDescriptor instances.

Most of the implementation is delegated to a dynamically defined manager class built by create_forward_many_to_many_manager() defined below.

get_previous_by_date_joined(*, field=<django.db.models.fields.DateTimeField: date_joined>, is_next=False, **kwargs)

class news.models.Publisher(*args, **kwargs)[source]

Bases: Model

Represents a news publishing organisation.

Stores publisher information and relationships with editors and journalists.

exception DoesNotExist

Bases: ObjectDoesNotExist

exception MultipleObjectsReturned

class news.models.ResetToken(*args, **kwargs)[source]

Bases: Model

Represents a password reset token for a users.

Tokens are generated during password reset requests and expire after a 5 minute period.

exception DoesNotExist

Bases: ObjectDoesNotExist

exception MultipleObjectsReturned

Bases: MultipleObjectsReturned

exception NotUpdated

Bases: ObjectNotUpdated, DatabaseError

news.serializers module

Serializers for converting model instances in JSON data.

class news.serializers.ArticleSerializer(*args, **kwargs)[source]

Bases: ModelSerializer

Serializes Article model data fro API responses.

class Meta[source]

Bases: object

fields = ['id', 'title', 'body', 'author', 'publisher', 'created_at']
model

alias of Article

news.tests module

Unit and integration tests for LNN News Applicaton.

class news.tests.ArticleAPITest(methodName='runTest')[source]

Bases: TestCase

Tests API functionality for subscribed article retireval.

setUp()[source]

Creates test readers, journalists, publishers and articles.

Sets up reusable test data for API endpoint testing.

test_api_returns_only_approved_articles()[source]

Tests that only approved articles are returned by the API.

Verifies: - authenticated access - filtering of approved articles - correct article serialization

class news.tests.ArticleModelTest(methodName='runTest')[source]

Bases: TestCase

Tests Article model creation and relationships.

test_article_creation()[source]

Tests successful creation of an article instance.

Verifies: - article title - author relationship - publisher relationship

class news.tests.UrlTests(methodName='runTest')[source]

Bases: TestCase

Tests application URL responses.

test_login_page()[source]

Tests that the login page loads successfully.

Returns:

None

Return type:

None

news.urls module

URL routes for the news application.

Defines routes for: - article management - dashboards - authentication - password reset flow - API endpoints

news.views module

Views for the LNN News Application.

This module contains: - Authentication views - Dashboard views - Article management views - Password reset functionality - Email helper utilities - Role-based access control

news.views.approve_article(request, article_id)[source]

Approves an article for publication.

Sends an email to subscribers and posts article updates to external API X(twitter).

Parameters:

  • request (HttpRequest) – HTTP request object

  • article_id (int) – Unique article identifier

Returns:

Redirect response to editor dashboard

Return type:

HttpResponseRedirect

news.views.build_email(user, reset_url)[source]

Builds the password reset email message.

Parameters:

  • user (CustomUser) – User requesting password reset

  • reset_url (str) – Secure password reset URL

Returns:

Configured email message object

Return type:

EmailMessage

news.views.create_article(request)[source]

Creates a new article submitted by a journalist.

Validates article form data and assigns the logged-in user as the author of the aticle.

Parameters:

request (HttpRequest) – HTTP request object

Returns:

Rendered article form or redirect response

Return type:

HttpResponse

news.views.create_newsletter(request)[source]

Creates a new newsletter submitted by a journalist.

Validates newsletter form data and assigns the logged-in user as the author.

Parameters:

request (HttpRequest) – HTTP request object

Returns:

Rendered newsletter form or redirect response

Return type:

HttpResponse

news.views.create_publisher(request)[source]

Creates a new publisher and assigns the logged-in editor to it.

Validates publisher form data and establishes the editor relationship.

Parameters:

request (HttpRequest) – HTTP request object

Returns:

Rendered publisher form or redirect response

Return type:

HttpResponse

news.views.delete_article(request, article_id)[source]

Deletes an existing article.

Parameters:

  • request (HttpRequest) – HTTP request object

  • article_id (int) – Unique article identifier

Returns:

Rendered confirmation page or redirect response

Return type:

HttpResponse

news.views.delete_newsletter(request, newsletter_id)[source]

Deletes an existing newsletter.

Parameters:

  • request (HttpRequest) – HTTP request object

  • article_id (int) – Unique newsletter identifier

Returns:

Rendered confirmation page or redirect response

Return type:

HttpResponse

news.views.edit_article(request, article_id)[source]

Edits an existing article.

Journalists only edit their own articles while editors may edit any article

Parameters:

  • request (HttpRequest) – HTTP request object

  • article_id (int) – Unique article identifier

Returns:

Rendered edit form or redirect response

Return type:

HttpResponse

news.views.edit_newsletter(request, newsletter_id)[source]

Edits an existing newsletter.

Journalists only edit their own articles while editors may edit any newsletter.

Parameters:

  • request (HttpRequest) – HTTP request object

  • article_id (int) – Unique newsletter identifier

Returns:

Rendered edit form or redirect response

Return type:

HttpResponse

news.views.editor_dashboard(request)[source]

Displays the editor dashboard, shows all articles created by a journalist.

Editor may read, update, delete all articles.

Parameters:

request (HttpRequest) – HTTP request object

Returns:

Rendered journalist dashboard

Return type:

HttpResponse

news.views.generate_reset_url(user)[source]

Generates a secure password reset URL.

Creates a hashed token with an expiry timestamp and stores it in the databse.

Parameters:

user (CustomUser) – User requesting password reset

Returns:

Secure password reset URL

Return type:

str

news.views.is_editor(user)[source]

Checks whether a user belongs to editor group.

Parameters:

user (CustomUser) – Authenticated user instance

Returns:

True if a user is an editor, otherwise False

Return type:

bool

news.views.is_journalist(user)[source]

Checks whether a user belongs to journalist group.

Parameters:

user (CustomUser) – Authenticated user instance

Returns:

True if a user is a journalist, otherwise False

Return type:

bool

news.views.is_reader(user)[source]

Checks whether a user belongs to reader group.

Parameters:

user (CustomUser) – Authenticated user instance

Returns:

True if a user is a reader, otherwise False

Return type:

bool

news.views.journalist_dashboard(request)[source]

Displays the journalist dashboard, shows all articles created by a journalist.

Journalist may create, read, update and delete their own articles

Parameters:

request (HttpRequest) – HTTP request object

Returns:

Rendered journalist dashboard

Return type:

HttpResponse

news.views.login_user(request)[source]

Authenticates and logs in a user.

Redirects authenticated users to dashboards based on their role.

Parameters:

request (HttpRequest) – Http request object

Returns:

Rendered login page or redirect response

Type:

HttpResponse

news.views.logout_user(request)[source]

Logs out a logged-in user.

Parameters:

request (HttpRequest) – Http request object

Returns:

Redirect response to login page

Return type:

HttpResponseRedirect

news.views.password_reset_request(request)[source]

Displays the password reset request form.

Parameters:

request – HttpRequest object

Returns:

Rendered password reset request template

news.views.reader_dashboard(request)[source]

Displays the journalist dashboard, shows all articles created by a journalist.

Reader may read articles.

Parameters:

request (HttpRequest) – HTTP request object

Returns:

Rendered journalist dashboard

Return type:

HttpResponse

news.views.register(request)[source]
news.views.reset_user_password(request, token)[source]

Resets a user’s password using a secure token.

Validates token existence and expiry before updating the user’s password.

Parameters:

  • request (HttpRequest) – Http request object

  • token (str) – Password reset token

Returns:

Redendered reset page or redirect response

Return type:

HttpResponse

news.views.review_articles(request)[source]
news.views.role_redirect(user)[source]
news.views.send_password_reset(request)[source]

Sends a password reset email to a user.

Generate a secure reset URL and emails the password reset instructions.

Parameters:

request (HttpRequest) – Http request object

Returns:

Redirect response to login page

Return type:

HttpResponseRedirect

news.views.welcome(request)[source]

Module contents
